title: Tennis Stars Practice at a Famous Soccer Stadium
summary: The Madrid Open is a big tennis tournament in Spain. This year, something special is happening. Real Madrid's Bernabeu stadium, which is usually for soccer, will have a tennis practice court. Top players like Carlos Alcaraz from Spain can use it to get ready for the tournament. The court will be like the ones at the main tournament site, but it's only for practice, not for matches. The tournament director, Feliciano Lopez, says it's exciting to have a tennis court in such an iconic stadium. Real Madrid doesn't have games that week, so the stadium is free. This helps players practice better and makes the Madrid Open more popular. It's not planned to have matches there in the future, just practice.

a famous soccer stadium in Madrid where Real Madrid plays
a tennis court where players can train before matches
a top Spanish tennis player who is number one in the world
the person in charge of the Madrid Open tournament

questions.question: Where will tennis players practice during the Madrid Open?
What will the Bernabeu stadium have for tennis players?
Will the Bernabeu host tournament matches?

background_read: The Madrid Open is an important tennis event in Spain, similar to big tournaments like the French Open. Real Madrid is a famous soccer team with a stadium called the Bernabeu, which is known worldwide. Tennis players often practice on special courts to improve their skills before competing. This year, the Madrid Open is using the Bernabeu to help players like Carlos Alcaraz get ready, making the tournament more exciting and visible to fans.
Article_Structure: WHO: Tennis players like Carlos Alcaraz and tournament director Feliciano Lopez
WHAT: The Bernabeu stadium will have a tennis practice court for the Madrid Open
WHERE: At the Bernabeu stadium in Madrid, Spain
WHY: To help players practice and increase the tournament's popularity

perspectives.description: Players get a good practice court in a famous stadium to prepare for matches
Using the Bernabeu makes the Madrid Open more visible and attracts attention
